title: Indian Shuttlers Need to Develop Independent On-Court Thinking
indian_angle: This opinion piece discusses the crucial need for Indian badminton players to develop self-reliance and strategic thinking during matches, especially without constant coaching intervention.
summary: An article emphasizes the importance for Indian badminton players to cultivate independent decision-making skills on court, enabling them to adapt and win even without direct coach guidance.

title: BAI Implements 3x15 Scoring System for Junior Domestic Events
indian_angle: This new scoring system will be implemented across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for U-13, U-15, and U-17 categories in India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has introduced a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, aiming to enhance competitive play.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Indian star Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing strong form on the international stage.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth's impressive run at the US Open concluded with a loss in the final, marking a significant performance for the Indian shuttler.

title: Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No. 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a major upset by defeating the sixth-ranked player in the world at the US Open.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, made headlines at the US Open by beating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen in a surprising victory.

title: Chennai to Host Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament
indian_angle: This circular announces a major upcoming domestic tournament, the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament, to be held in Chennai.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has released a circular detailing the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026, scheduled to take place in Chennai from July 16th to 23rd.

title: India Invites EOI to Host BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: India is actively pursuing the opportunity to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in 2026, signaling its growing prominence in global badminton.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest for hosting the BWF World Championships 2026, indicating a strong bid to bring the global event to India.
